title: pilosella cymosa subsp. sabina
intro: Pilosella cymosa subsp. sabina, also known as Sabine's mouse-ear or Sabine's hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pilosella cymosa subsp. sabina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Pilosella cymosa subsp. sabina can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to make herbal remedies for various health conditions.
